messages.content: Build a safe nested-property getter in fp-ts 2.x using Option chain. Given an optional user record with an optional address, return the uppercased city or "UNKNOWN" when any hop is missing.
Convert this null-check ladder to fp-ts 2.x Option chain: `const first = users[0]; const email = first == null ? 'none' : (first.email == null ? 'none' : first.email.toLowerCase());`
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to look up a key in a Map and default when absent? Show a minimal example using Option chain.
Implement a safe integer parser in fp-ts 2.x that returns Option<number> and chains into a divisor guard, defaulting to -1 when parsing fails or the value is zero.
Explain briefly how Option chain works in fp-ts 2.x, then show a typed implementation that extracts the domain part of an email or returns 'anonymous'.
Write an fp-ts 2.x Option chain that finds the first active subscription for a user and returns its plan name, defaulting to 'free'.
Show me the fp-ts 2.x equivalent of `user?.profile?.avatarUrl ?? '/default.png'` using Option chain. Brief example.
Build a nullable-safe config reader in fp-ts 2.x. Given process.env, extract PORT as a number between 1024 and 65535, defaulting to 3000 when missing or out of range.
Add a fallback-logging step to this fp-ts 2.x pipeline without breaking the Option chain: `pipe(O.fromNullable(id), O.map((x) => x.trim()))`. The default should be 'guest'.
Implement Option chain in fp-ts 2.x with strict types — no `any`, all steps typed — for a function that returns the author name of the first comment on a post.
Fix this fp-ts 2.x fragment to use Option chain properly — right now it returns `Option<Option<string>>`: `pipe(O.fromNullable(u), O.map((x) => O.fromNullable(x.email)))`
Build a safe head-and-transform helper in fp-ts 2.x that takes a readonly array and a transform, returning the transformed first element or a default.
Write an fp-ts 2.x Option chain that parses a JSON string, extracts the `version` field as a string, and defaults to '0.0.0' on any failure.
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to safely access an array by index and transform the element? Show a minimal example.
Refactor this fp-ts 2.x code to remove the nested calls and use Option chain via pipe: `O.getOrElse(() => 'x')(O.map((s: string) => s.trim())(O.fromNullable(input)))`
Implement an fp-ts 2.x Option chain that looks up a feature flag by name, defaulting to `false` when the flag is missing or its value is not a boolean.
Show me the fp-ts 2.x equivalent of chained optional chaining with a default: `obj?.a?.b?.c ?? 'fallback'`. Brief example.
Build an fp-ts 2.x Option chain that reads a header from a `Headers`-like object, lowercases it, and short-circuits on missing/empty values with a default of 'application/octet-stream'.
Write an fp-ts 2.x function with Option chain that, given a potentially undefined date string, returns the year as a number or 0 on any failure.
Write an fp-ts 2.x Option chain that safely extracts a nested numeric field (cart.totals.grandTotal) and formats it as USD, falling back to '$0.00' on any missing hop.
Build an email validator in fp-ts 2.x using Either chain. Accumulate three rules (non-empty, contains '@', under 254 chars) with short-circuit semantics and return Either<string, string>.

Convert this throw-based parsing code to fp-ts 2.x Either chain: `function parse(s: string) { const n = Number(s); if (Number.isNaN(n)) throw new Error('nan'); if (n < 0) throw new Error('neg'); return n; }`
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to chain Eithers with different error types? Show a minimal example using E.chainW.
Implement an fp-ts 2.x Either chain that validates and normalises a username — must be 3–20 chars, alphanumeric-only, lowercased — and folds the result to a string.
Explain briefly how Either chain works in fp-ts 2.x, then show a typed implementation that safely divides two parsed numeric strings.
Show me the fp-ts 2.x equivalent of a try/catch chain that decodes, validates, and saves a record. Use Either chain with E.mapLeft to normalise errors to a string.
Build an fp-ts 2.x Either pipeline that parses a config string of the form 'k=v;k=v;...' into Record<string,string>, short-circuiting on the first malformed pair.
Write an fp-ts 2.x Either chain that validates a JWT-ish token structure (three base64 parts) and returns the decoded payload or a tagged error.
Refactor this to use fp-ts 2.x Either chain with E.match to produce a single return string: a two-step parse-then-normalise pipeline over `input: string`.
Implement fp-ts 2.x Either chain with strict types — no `any`, all events typed — for a two-stage pipeline: parse a string as a positive int, then multiply by a factor, folding to a status code.

Fix this fp-ts 2.x fragment so the types compile — the error channels don't unify with plain E.chain: `pipe(parseA(s), E.chain(validateB))` where parseA returns Either<'parse', A> and validateB returns Either<'invalid', B>.
Write an fp-ts 2.x Either chain that validates credit card fields (number length, expiry format, cvv digits) and folds to an HTTP status code.
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to transform only the error side of an Either? Show a minimal example using E.mapLeft.
Build an fp-ts 2.x Either chain for URL parsing: split off the protocol, host, and path, returning a tagged union error on any failure.
Show the fp-ts 2.x equivalent of chained async-looking validations with sync semantics. Use Either chain with E.fold at the edge.
Implement an fp-ts 2.x Either chain that parses an ISO date string, then subtracts it from `now`, returning the difference in days or a tagged error.
Write an fp-ts 2.x Either chain that validates a percentage (0-100 inclusive) from a string, applying E.mapLeft to produce a user-facing message and E.fold to a default number.
Build an fp-ts 2.x Either chain that composes three validators and reports the winning error tag via E.match. The input is an object with fields `id`, `email`, `age`.
Show me the fp-ts 2.x equivalent of a promise-chain `.then/.catch` but synchronous: three sequential steps that can each fail with their own tagged error, folded to a single result.
Implement an fp-ts 2.x Either chain with strict types for an HTTP middleware: require an auth header, verify its shape, then resolve a user id.
